items.name: Dice set
items.description: This item encompasses a wide range of game pieces, including dice and decks of cards (for games such as Three-Dragon Ante). A few common examples appear on the Tools table, but other kinds of gaming sets exist. If you are proficient with a gaming set, you can add your proficiency bonus to ability checks you make to play a game with that set. Each type of gaming set requires a separate proficiency.

items.name: Pouch
items.description: A cloth or leather pouch can hold up to 20 sling bullets or 50 blowgun needles, among other things. A compartmentalized pouch for holding spell components is called a component pouch (described earlier in this section).

traits: I'm full of inspiring and cautionary tales from my military experience relevant to almost every combat situation.
I'm haunted by memories of war. I can't get the images of violence out of my mind.
I've lost too many friends, and I'm slow to make new ones.
I can stare down a hell hound without flinching.
I enjoy being strong and like breaking things.
I have a crude sense of humor.
I face problems head-on. A simple, direct solution is the best path to success.
ideals: Greater Good. Our lot is to lay down our lives in defense of others. (Good)
Responsibility. I do what must be done and follow orders, even if I don't like them. (Lawful)
Independence. When people follow orders blindly, they embrace a kind of tyranny. (Chaotic)
Might. In life as in war, the stronger force prevails. (Evil)
Live and Let Live. Ideals aren't worth killing over or going to war for. (Neutral)
Nation. My city, nation, or people are all that matter. (Any)
bonds: I would still lay down my life for the people I served with.
Someone saved my life on the battlefield. To this day, I will never leave a friend behind.
I'll never forget the crushing defeat my company suffered or the enemies who dealt it.
Those who fight beside me are those worth dying for.
I fight for those who cannot fight for themselves.
flaws: The monstrous enemy we faced in battle still leaves me quivering with fear.
I have little respect for anyone who is not a proven warrior.
I made a terrible mistake in battle that cost many lives, and I would do anything to keep that mistake secret.
My hatred of my enemies is blind and unreasoning.
I obey the law, even if the law causes misery.
I'd rather eat my armor than admit when I'm wrong.

activeQuests.title: The First Step
activeQuests.description: You awake in a strange clearing. The air is fresh, but the silence is heavy. You must get your bearings.

objectives.description: Master the Booklet: View all pages (Character, Map, Quests, Equipment, Codex)
Study Your Gear: Examine an item in your inventory
Begin the Journey: Move to a neighboring hex

storySummary: started their journey as a Human Fighter with the Soldier background.
lastNarrative: Goblin looses an arrow that hits TestHero with a thud. (1 damage)

conversationHistory.content:
Your eyes flutter open to a sky brushed with the pale gold of early morning. The air is crisp, carrying the faint scent of damp grass and distant pine. Around you stretches an endless sea of rolling plains, their grasses swaying like a green ocean in a gentle breeze. To the south, the land thins into a darker, tangled mass—an uncharted jungle that seems to swallow the horizon. To the north, jagged silhouettes rise, the promise of mountains cloaked in mist. Far off to the northwest and north, low hills roll like sleeping beasts, while the far‑se southeast remains a flat, open plain indistinguishable from the ground you stand on.

You have no recollection of how you arrived here; only your name—TestHero—echoes in your mind. A shiver runs down your spine as a distant, mournful howl ripples across the plains, its source hidden beyond the line of sight, hinting at unseen dangers lurking in the wild.

You can:
1. **Survey the surroundings** more closely, searching for tracks, footprints, or any sign of recent activity.
2. **Head toward the distant howl**, following the sound to its source, though it lies far beyond the immediate plain.
3. **Move toward a notable landmark**—the jungle to the south, the mountains to the north, or the hills to the northwest—to get a better sense of the terrain and perhaps find shelter.

The morning holds both wonder and a faint unease, as the plains whisper of untold stories while the howl warns that you are not alone.

You turn north and stride across the rolling grass of the open plains. The wind carries the scent of wildflowers and dry earth, and the blades of grass sway in unison under the high sun. After a short walk, you arrive at a modest clearing that bears the marks of your first arrival in this region. Scattered footprints lead back toward the south, and a broken lance half-buried in the soil hints at a past skirmish. A weathered wooden sign, half covered in dust, stands upright, its lettering faded but still legible: 'Initial Landing Site'. No other souls are visible, and the surrounding plains stretch out in all directions, inviting further exploration.

The high sun beats down on the endless sea of grasses, each stalk rustling softly in the warm breeze. You shift your weight, easing into a slower, quieter gait, the weight of your armor whispering against the blades. Though the open plains offer little natural cover, the tall grasses provide enough sway and shadow to mask your movements from a distance. You keep your shield low and your sword sheathed, minimizing the metallic clank that might give you away. As you pace stealthily northward, the distant horizon remains a blur of plain and hill, and with no eyes watching you at the moment, you manage to blend into the wavering sea of green, though the bright daylight makes true concealment difficult.

(If you wish to determine how effectively you remain unseen, a Dexterity (Stealth) check may be called for.)

You adjust your stride to a steady, normal pace, feeling the warm midday sun on your back as you move across the vast expanse of plains. The ground beneath your boots is firm and dry, dotted with occasional tufts of hardy grass that sway gently in the light breeze. To the south, the dense jungle looms, its canopy a dark, mottled green against the bright sky. To the north, the plain continues unbroken, offering a clear line of sight toward distant horizons. The air is filled with the faint scent of wildflowers and the distant call of a lone hawk circling overhead. With your pace set, you can take your time to observe the surroundings or press onward as you wish.

You scout the open expanse of the plains for a spot that offers a little shelter from the wind. A gentle rise of golden grass catches your eye, its tall stalks swaying softly in the cold evening breeze. The distant calls of jungle creatures drift faintly from the south and west, but the immediate area feels relatively quiet, giving you a brief reprieve from any immediate danger.

You settle onto the soft grass, leaning against a solitary, weather‑worn stone that serves as a makeshift backrest. The sky is a muted gray, the low winter sun casting long shadows across the plain. Taking a moment to catch your breath, you decide to take a short rest. You close your eyes, focus on your breathing, and mentally count your remaining hit dice.

Spending one hit die, you manage to staunch the worst of your wounds. The lingering sting of pain eases as you feel a modest surge of vitality return to your body. Though still bearing the marks of battle, you are now better prepared to continue your journey.

After a brief period of quiet contemplation, you rise, feeling a little stronger. The plains stretch out before you in all directions—jungle to the south and west, more open grassland to the north and east—awaiting your next step.

codexEntries.title: The Veiled Terror of the Shattered Vale
codexEntries.content:
**Physical Description**
The NonExistentMonster is spoken of only in whispers, for no eye has ever truly beheld its full form. Survivors describe a shifting silhouette that seems to flicker between the outline of a colossal serpentine beast and a mass of writhing shadows. Its skin, if it can be called that, appears to be composed of a semi‑translucent membrane that absorbs ambient light, rendering the creature nearly invisible in twilight. When it moves, ripples of darkness cascade across its body like oil on water, and occasional glints of phosphorescent veins pulse with an eerie violet hue.

**Biology**
Scholars hypothesize that the NonExistentMonster is an aberration of planar energy, a convergence of the Shadowfell and the Elemental Plane of Air. Its musculature is said to be composed of tightly packed etheric filaments that can contract without the need for conventional nerves, allowing it to glide silently through the air or slither across solid ground. The creature’s diet is rumored to consist of ambient magical resonance; it feeds on lingering spells, ley‑line currents, and the lingering fear of living beings, converting emotional energy into sustenance.

**Ecology**
The beast is believed to haunt the ruins of the Shattered Vale, a region where the veil between worlds grew thin after the Great Sundering. Here, the NonExistentMonster stalks the ruins of ancient citadels, nesting within the collapsed arches where ley‑lines intersect. It is said to patrol a territory roughly the size of a small kingdom, maintaining a domain of perpetual dusk. The presence of the creature correlates with sudden drops in magical potency and an increase in unexplained disappearances.

**Myths and Legends**
Legends claim the NonExistentMonster was born from the grief of a forgotten deity, cast out and transformed into a living void. Bards recount a prophecy that the monster will awaken fully when the moon is eclipsed thrice in a single night, at which point it will devour the sun itself. Some cultures view it as a guardian of forbidden knowledge, believing that only those who truly understand the balance of light and shadow may approach it without peril.

**Encounter Notes**
Adventurers who have survived an encounter advise extreme caution. The monster’s ability to blend with darkness renders conventional sight useless; magical illumination or true‑sight spells are recommended. Its attacks are described as tendrils of pure entropy that can unravel armor and sap life force. Creatures that manage to strike its phosphorescent veins with radiant energy seem to cause it great pain, temporarily dispersing its form into harmless shadows.

**Survivor’s Tale**
"I thought the night itself had turned against me. The air grew cold, and the darkness seemed to breathe. When the thing emerged, I could feel my very thoughts being swallowed. I raised my holy torch, and for a heartbeat the creature recoiled, its form cracking like glass. I ran, heart pounding, never looking back. To this day, the memory of that violet glow haunts my dreams—still, I carry the torch, for I know that light is the only thing that can ever truly chase it away." – Aric Stormwarden, Ranger of the Silver Keep
